experiment: A/B/C/D runtime sonar visibility of the generated outfall

engine.octree_rebuild_log_line: HolodeckSonar: world geometry changed, rebuilding octree.

level: ExampleLevel (fork-internal level; NOT an official Ocean world - superseded by the official-level phase)
outfall_geometry: see scene_manifest.json (93 components: approach pipeline, flanges, berm, transition collar, diffuser, sleepers, 6 risers + collars + alternating nozzles, end cap; scatter rocks disabled)
poses: 6 poses: bearings 0/90/180/270 deg at 18 m + 45/225 deg at 28 m from the diffuser centre, z = bed + 3 m, camera aimed at the centre; teleport-pinned each tick

conditions.B: outfall via legacy SpawnProp blueprint props (NOT removable at runtime)
conditions.C: outfall via fork SpawnAsset static meshes (octree marked dirty and rebuilt)
conditions.D: ClearSpawned (removes SpawnAsset actors only; B props persist by design of the legacy path)

known_contamination: B-phase legacy props cannot be removed within a session, so their acoustic signature persists into C and D. C-B isolates the SpawnAsset contribution. The clean re-run with independent engine sessions per condition is scheduled in the official-level phase.
command: python scripts/validate_custom_sonar.py  (engine first via python scripts/launch_custom_engine.py)

files.report.json: per-pose mean|diff| + bit-identical flags per phase pair + detector contacts on C frames
files.raw_frames/frames_{A,B,C,D}_*.npz: raw 512x256 sonar frames per pose per condition
files.scene_manifest.json: exact spawned component list (type, location, rotation encoding, scale, material)

headline_findings: C differs from A at 6/6 poses; structured signature with acoustic shadows at structure-consistent ranges; detector contacts up to 38k bins
on the fork, legacy spawn_prop props are ALSO acoustically visible (B-A large): the fork handles runtime props generally
global mean|frame diff| is noise/jitter-dominated (~0.014 for all phases) and is NOT the evidence; localized contrast (up to 17x head-on) and difference images are
